title: Omalotheca supina
uses: Omalotheca supina, also known as common woolly hair grass, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness in treating various health conditions. Some traditional uses of Omalotheca supina include its application as a wound treatment and an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's extracts have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which could be beneficial in preventing infections and promoting tissue repair. Further studies are required to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Omalotheca supina.
taxonomy: Omalotheca supina is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other members of the family, such as its composite flower head and the presence of ray and disc florets. The genus Omalotheca is a group of plants that are typically annual or biennial and are native to Europe and Asia. The species O. supina is further classified within the genus based on its unique characteristics, including its leaf shape and flower size. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Omalotheca supina is closely related to other plants in this family, such as daisies and sunflowers.
benefits: Omalotheca supina is known for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues and skin problems. Its extracts have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a useful agent against infections. Omalotheca supina may also have anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm this. Additionally, its leaves and stems are rich in vitamins and minerals, providing nutritional value when consumed.
properties: Omalotheca supina has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Research suggests that extracts from this plant may possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. The plant's compounds have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. constituents: Omalotheca supina contains flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes as its active constituents, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Flavonoids present in Omalotheca supina, such as apigenin and luteolin, are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Terpenoids and sesquiterpenes in the plant are believed to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The specific combination and concentration of these compounds may vary depending on factors like the plant's growing conditions and geographical location.

intro: Omalotheca supina, also known as the creeping cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Omalotheca supina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Omalotheca supina are tea, tincture, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
